Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RICHARD SANDOVAL Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or In The Alternative Tax Costs

PLAINTIFF RICHARD SANDOVAL's Motion To Strike Or In The Alternative Tax Costs.

Defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc. (AHM) won a lemon law jury trial, then sought its costs by memorandum. Plaintiff moved to strike or tax certain of the costs. (Not. 1:9-14.) Plaintiff's motion is adjudged as follows.

Filing fees ($954). Motions need not be granted to be "reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation." (CCP 1033.5(c).) Plaintiff's motion is denied.

Deposition charges ($1,200). AHM provides insufficient support for this amount of deposition charges. (Opp. 5:8-16.) Plaintiff's motion is granted.

Service of process charges ($365). AHM provides insufficient support for this amount of these charges. (Opp. 5:17-24.) Plaintiff's motion is granted.

Expert fees ($14,042). Plaintiff rejected AMH's $10,000 CCP 998 offer, thus opening himself to post-offer expert fees. That is not a token offer in a single-vehicle lemon law case. Moreover, the AHM expert's hourly rate ($381) and time worked (31 hours) were reasonable. Plaintiff's motion is denied.

Other charges ($8,522). The court exercises its discretion to strike these charges not authorized by statute. Plaintiff's motion is granted.

In sum, AHM's cost memorandum is stricken/taxed $10,087.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
